patient_profile_summary.present_illness_positive: 2-day history of severe periumbilical abdominal pain; gradual onset; slowly worsened; waxing and waning quality; worst every 5-60 minutes; spastic quality; up to 10/10 severity; current pain 8/10; caused anorexia; caused sleeplessness; nausea; light vomiting that is non-bilious and non-bloody; significant belching; similar episodes of diffuse periumbilical pain for years with increasing frequency, including 3 episodes this past year; associated constipation with episodes; constipation occasionally resolves to diarrhea; intentional weight loss over the past month due to diet and exercise; fatigue; malaise
patient_profile_summary.medical_history: Atrial fibrillation status post ablation treatment; obstructive sleep apnea; gout; pulmonary embolus; DVT; essential hypertension; benign prostatic hyperplasia; diverticulosis; broken nose; open right inguinal herniorrhaphy; ruptured Achilles tendon repair

turns.doctor: Hello, I'm your doctor today. Can you tell me what brought you to the ED?
turns.patient: I have bad stomach pain, and I've been throwing up.

turns.doctor: Can you tell me about your past medical history?
turns.patient: I have atrial fibrillation, sleep apnea, gout, and high blood pressure. I also had a pulmonary embolus and DVT. I have diverticulosis and benign prostatic hyperplasia. I broke my nose before, and had surgery for a hernia and my Achilles tendon.

turns.doctor: Do you live alone or with someone?
turns.patient: I live with my wife. This pain is really not getting better.

turns.doctor: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I quit smoking many years ago. I drink wine or beer sometimes, maybe one or two a week. The pain... it's still pretty bad.

turns.doctor: What medications do you usually take?
turns.patient: I take metoprolol, tamsulosin, and Xarelto. My stomach really hurts.
It started two days ago. It's not getting better. I'm worried about this.
